So you need to unemotionally write your business. I mentioned this a few different times, but I figured operationally you need an entire course on this specific video. When I say this, I'm not saying in a sense that you don't put passion into your sales, that you don't put emotion into the positivity that you preach when you're talking to people inside of your organization. I'm not saying that you don't put emotion into things that require it when necessary to derive a feeling or an emotion response from people when necessary. I'm simply saying that when you're making decisions such as firing people, hiring people, getting people in a position where you're running off of statistics instead of having an emotional bias inside your organization or favoritism or nepotism, you need to understand that unemotionally running your business is going to be the greatest way for you to get a lot of results. It's going to put you in a position where you can dominate and scale immensely past the point you would be able to if you emotionally limited yourself. Even though sometimes we feel like emotionally running our business is the best choice, it's actually always the worst. It always puts us in a position where we create limiting beliefs because emotion is a secondary part of our brain. It goes our fear part, which is the amygdala to the limbic system to our frontal cortex, which is where logic occurs. So if you're a female, unfortunately, you go on a position where you have a little bit of a bias here because brains are wired differently. Brains are wired differently. Our brain chemistry is different when it's male versus female. Brains do have more of a logical approach. So males have more of a perspective where this is female and this is point B. We go in the most logical route to get to that point. That being said, if this is a female logic, females get to the same point where they're typically a little more loose and they get to the point in the sense of little more creativity and flow. Both get to point A and point B. Both get to the exact same amount of time typically, then it's not more logic, which can have a little more unemotional bias because we have more direction. That being said, all you have to do is understand what's getting the amygdala to the limbic system to the frontal cortex to the amygdala to the limbic system where emotion occurs, to the limbic system where logic occurs, where you can, if you bypass your emotional system, go right into the logic where you can have the clearest direction and the best possible perspective on executing things inside of your business. That being said, all I want you to do is sometimes when you get a little worked up, take a deep breath, calm down, chill out a little bit, take a step back, and before you make a decision like firing somebody because you're all upset or firing a client because they're going to guard you past 6pm or because you can't really deliver or was open and you get all upset and flustered, stop. That's all I want you to do.
